# Golf Match™ — Official Instructions Reference

> Transcribed from the Instructions tab of the Golf Match™ Excel workbook.

---

## Introduction

**Golf Match™** expedites the "figuring out and settling-up" process that traditionally follows a round of golf when multiple foursomes and/or threesomes and/or fivesomes are participating in various types of competitions. While expediting the settling-up process, **Golf Match™** also eliminates calculation and other human errors that are often the result of "calling scores" or performing "manual calculations."

**Golf Match™** is set up in worksheets that calculate various types of competitions. All worksheets include instructions for easy use. And many worksheet cells (those with red triangles in the upper right hand corner) include guidance comments which appear when the mouse pointer hovers above such cells.

Before beginning to use **Golf Match™**, tour the program by "clicking" on the various worksheets below. (Arrows in the bottom left hand corner of Excel assist in moving through all **Golf Match™** worksheets.) All worksheets are populated with sample data so the program may be seen in action.

**IMPORTANT:** On all worksheets, the user may only enter preference selections [check boxes, choices, etc.] or enter information into the **light blue worksheet cells.** *All other worksheet areas are "protected" and prohibit user entry, ...so basically, you can't screw anything up.*

If information in any light blue cell is changed, or if any preference selection is changed, **Golf Match™** will instantly recalculate the results. On your tour, make some changes and watch what happens.

(For slower computers, Round Robin and Combo Bets may not function. Also, older slower computers take longer to recalculate everything. Consequently, a user may choose to switch from Automatic Calculation to Manual Calculation by clicking Tools, Options, Calculation tab, Manual. Recalculation will then only take place when the user presses the F9 key, therein eliminating constant recalculations and speeding up data input.)

Once you've concluded your tour, the following instructions, as well as those found on each worksheet, become your guide.

---

## Set Up Your Golf Match Template

Simply follow the below steps, and you'll then be ready to "personalize" your **Golf Match Template**.

1. Using Windows Explorer, create a **Golf Match™** folder in a convenient location on your computer hard drive.

2. Within the **Golf Match™** folder, create a second folder entitled **Template**.

3. Within the **Template** folder, create a third folder entitled **History**.

4. Next, with the **Golf Match™** program loaded in Excel, click the Excel File menu, click Save As..., double click the newly created **Golf Match™** folder, and click Save. (You have now saved the program with all sample data included, for your future reference, if needed.)

5. Next, with the **Golf Match™** program still loaded in Excel, click the Excel File menu, click Save As, double click the newly-created **Template** folder, rename the file **Golf Match Template** and click Save.

6. Now, with the newly named **Golf Match Template** still loaded in Excel, click each successive **Golf Match™** worksheet tab (e.g. "Courses," "Players-Scores," "Foursomes," etc.) and eliminate the sample data in each worksheet by simply clicking the "Clear Form For New Entry" button.

7. Next, click Save and you're **Golf Match Template** is ready, so you may proceed to the next step: "...Personalize your Golf Match Template."

---

## Personalize your Golf Match Template

With your **Golf Match Template** open in Excel, click the "Courses" worksheet, and enter the information requested for the particular course or courses your group regularly plays.

Next, click the "Players-Scores" worksheet. In alphabetical order, enter each **Player Name**, (last then first) for the players who regularly play in your group. Next, enter each player's approximate **Handicap Index**, **Nickname**, **Home Course Number** and **Handicap Card Number**. (**Home Course Number** and **Handicap Card Number** are not necessary to operate **Golf Match™**, but for honest and efficient score-reporting purposes, as you will read below, they are important. You can input them when you get them, so don't stop now.)

Next, click the "Foursomes" worksheet, and make selections and complete bet amounts based upon your group's current preferences and norms. (Unquestionably, your group will not likely play all forms of competitions available just yet, but you may grow into many of them, particularly since they are fun and can now be figured so quickly and accurately. And if the group keeps honest handicaps, and plays by the rules of golf, the dough pretty much goes in a circle! All **Golf Match™** competitions, games, and options are fully explained below.) Next, if desired, complete the same information on the "Foursomes Round Robin", "Threesomes", "Fivesomes" or any other worksheets which you know will be regularly used.

Finally, return to the "Players-Scores" worksheet cell A1, and click the Excel Save Icon (small computer disk), and close your **Golf Match Template**.

Now, whenever you open your **Golf Match Template**, your regular players, course(s), group preferences and typical bet amounts will all be waiting. And furthermore, the program will open to the Players-Scores worksheet, ready for scores for the day's round. (And yes, anything can still be changed within the **Golf Match Template**; simply make the desired changes then once again save as **Golf Match Template**.)

---

## Handicaps and Equitable Stroke Control

As an added convenience, the "Players – Scores" worksheet applies the USGA **E**quitable **S**troke **C**ontrol system to each player's score, based on handicap, and calculates the adjusted score each player should report for handicap purposes. With each player's **ESC Adjusted Score**, **Home Course Number** and **Handicap Card Number**, entry of scores for USGA or State run handicap systems is easy. In fact, some groups merely print and hand the completed "Players -- Scores" worksheet to the pro-shop, and the pro-shop personnel enter all scores for the group! (ESC score entry by the Pro Shop helps keep player handicaps accurate and honest.)

**ESC Adjusted Scores** are merely provided for your convenience and have no bearing on bets calculated by **Golf Match™**.

---

## Figuring Bets and Saving Today's Round

**Golf Match™** calculates various types of traditional (as well as some non-traditional, but fun) golf bets.

As mentioned above, once each player's handicap and hole by hole scores are accurately entered, merely select the tab which represents the type of bet to be figured, make (or double check) preference selections (choose handicapping method, type of play, automatic presses, etc.), input wager amounts, player numbers, and viola: instant correct results. Specific instructions may be found on each individual worksheet, and complete explanations for all **Golf Match™** bets follow this section. (Again, use the arrows in the bottom left hand corner of the screen, adjacent to the "Instructions" tab, to quickly move from worksheet to worksheet.)

**Special Tip #5:** Always prioritize calculations and printed results based upon which bets include the most participants. (Some guys have got to go!) "Foursome", "Foursome Round Robin", "Threesome" and/or "Fivesome" bets normally concern everyone; they should come first. "Indo--Round Robin" and "Best Ball--Round Robin" bets as well as "Skins" bets (gross and/or net) would probably come next. Thereafter, you're on your own.

**Special Tip #6:** Hyperlinks are found on many **Golf Match™** worksheets. When you "click" on a hyperlink, you are instantly taken to another area of **Golf Match™** which further explains in detail what you were viewing when you clicked the hyperlink. For example, on the "Individual Bets" worksheet, "click" on the Match # >>> **5** to view the actual hole-by-hole score card and match between the participants in Match #5. To instantly be back where you started, "Click" the **"Return to Input View"** button. You will find numerous hyperlinks in **Golf Match™** which will aid your navigation. (When your mouse pointer becomes a pointing finger, you are hovering above a hyperlink.)

**VERY IMPORTANT: Saving Today's Round.** After entering scores and figuring bets, your **Golf Match Template** will be filled with data which **should not** be Saved in the **Golf Match Template** on your computer hard drive. ...But, you will want to save today's round. Sooooooo, click the Excel File menu, click Save As, select the **History** folder (see "Set Up" above), rename the file with the date (year first) and course played, such as "08-09-21 Pine Valley CC" and click Save. The day's round is now saved in your **History** folder, ...but your **Golf Match Template** remains unchanged on your hard drive, ready for your next round. (In using year first in the file name, your saved days will always remain in chronological order.)

**Saving FYI:** Each day saved in your **History** folder is simply a copy of the **Golf Match™** program, loaded with today's info.

Following this section, you will find complete explanations for each **Golf Match™** worksheet, function and type of bet. Read 'em, try 'em, play 'em, and send us your feedback and questions to **TechSupport@GolfMatch.US**.

Also, as suggested above, print and review the **Golf Match Tips and Shortcuts** file. It will make your entire **Golf Match™** experience all the more fantastico!

---

## Foursomes, Threesomes and Fivesomes

The Foursomes, Threesomes and Fivesomes worksheets calculate competition between foursomes and/or between threesomes and/or between fivesomes. (Brilliant.) Teams should be drawn so that skill levels and aggregate team handicaps are as equal as possible, and up to 15 teams may be included. The Foursomes, Threesomes and Fivesomes worksheets calculate various bets between such teams.

### 1) Nassau

The Nassau is a bet in which one or more low net scores for each foursome/threesome/fivesome for each hole are totaled for the front nine, the back nine and the eighteen and are compared with the front, back and eighteen hole scores of each opposing team. The number of low net scores which count on each hole is a preference selection on the Foursomes/Threesomes/Fivesomes worksheets. (Concurrent Nassaus are calculated in the Foursomes Round Robin worksheet. See below.)

### 2) Investment

Numerous Investment bet options are available. A simple Investment bet requires at least one member of a team to score a net par or better on each hole in order to stay "on" the Investment. A team is considered "off" the Investment any time none of its players succeed in scoring a net par or better on a hole. Each "off" costs each team member the amount of the Investment bet multiplied by the number of opposing teams. (For Foursomes/Threesomes, two alternative simple investment bet variations are also available: 1 -- Two net pars or better in order to stay "on," and 2 -- Two net pars to stay "on" and one net par to stay "on," therein making it possible for a team to go "off" twice on any one hole. For Fivesomes, any and all combinations of up to three net pars per hole may be selected, therein making it possible for a team to go "off" up to three times on any hole. Ouch.)

#### 2-a) "Investment Redemption"

"Investment Redemption" provides the opportunity to recoup losses after going "off" when playing the above Investment Bets. Investment Redemption only applies to a team which has gone "off" and is in position to lose money on Investment Bet(s), and works as follows: once a team goes "off," if on any remaining hole, all team members score a net par or better ("4 Ons" for Foursomes, etc.), each team member wins back the designated amount multiplied by the number of opposing teams. (If "offs" are $4 each, and Redemptions are $2 each, two Redemptions would be necessary to offset one "off.") Investment Redemption only allows a team to recoup Investment Bet losses *after* going "off," not earn dough by achieving additional "4 Ons/3 Ons/5 Ons" during the round. Also, Redemptions may not be "banked" to offset future "offs." And obviously, if a team goes "off" on hole #18, there is no Redemption; you're out of real estate. Redemption works exactly the same for Foursomes, Threesomes and Fivesomes. In addition, Investment Redemption does not apply to the below Dunn Investment Option.

#### 2-b) "4 Ons Bet" ("3 Ons Bet" for Threesomes, "5 Ons Bet" for Fivesomes)

"4 Ons Bet" may be selected as an alternative to Investment Redemption. If "4 Ons Bet" is chosen in Foursomes, ANY TIME all four team members score a net par or better on any hole ("4 Ons"), each team member wins the designated amount multiplied by the number of opposing teams. This bet may be selected in conjunction with the above Investment Bets, but may also be played independently regardless of whether or not any Investment Bets are selected.

#### 2-c) The "DUNN Investment"

The "DUNN Investment" is an additional variation and is herein fondly named after its avid advocate, Jimmy Dunn, a gentlemen and true golfaholic extraordinaire. The DUNN Investment is slightly tricky at first glance, but easily understandable upon examination. It may be played in concert with the previously described investment games, and is typically played for an amount more substantial than the mere "off" amount. The Dunn Investment is lost if/when one team fails, on any hole, to meet the selected "investment" requirement (determined in the regular Investment bet). Should one team go "off" (even multiple times), and an opposing team NOT go "off," the first team would lose the DUNN Investment amount, as well as the designated amount for each "off." Alternatively, should one team go "off" (even more than once) followed by an opposing team subsequently going "off" on a later hole, then both teams are considered "off," and a new DUNN Investment bet of the same original amount is considered to immediately begin on the hole following which team #2 went "off." The "DUNN" difference is that it is immaterial whether or not team #1 goes "off" repeatedly before team number #2 ultimately goes "off." Simply, and once again, a new investment bet of the original DUNN Investment amount begins again on the hole subsequent to the hole on which team #2 goes off. In the DUNN Investment mode, the DUNN Investment amount is paid in addition to the cost for each "off." (Numerous possibilities exist, but **Golf Match™** accurately sorts it all out electronically.)

### 3) Strokes

The Strokes bet compares net strokes between teams. Strokes for all team members are totaled, handicap strokes are deducted and the resulting sum is compared with the corresponding sum for every other team. (In order to "limit" the possible loss on the strokes bet, some groups limit the highest maximum net score per player. This option is provided by **Golf Match™**.)

---

## Deduction for Group Expenses

Should a group desire to deduct and pay certain "group expenses" from amounts won, the Foursomes/Threesomes/Fivesomes/Foursomes Round Robin/Indo Tourney worksheets provide this capability. Merely enter the percentage to be deducted from the winners' take in the "Deduction % from Winnings:" box. **Golf Match™** will then deduct this % from each winning player/team and the resulting total deduction will appear in the "Total $ Deducted from Winners" box. Upon collection and payout, this amount should be collected and accounted for by the group treasurer. Examples of "group expenses" may include costs for computer, printer, **Golf Match™**, supplies and depreciation, flower fund for family/friend funerals, year-end party, awards, tips for pro-shop personnel, beverage fund for guests, computer operator (eh?), etc.

The Foursomes, Threesomes and Fivesomes worksheets recreate a scorecard for each team that shows each player's net scores, the running as well as front/back/18 hole Nassau scores, Investment "offs", Investment Redemptions (or the "4, 3 or 5 Ons" Bet), and the Strokes calculation for each team. To see any team's specific information, click the team's name (e.g., **"#2. Gipetto's team >>>>>"**) in the first column, and a hyperlink will instantly take you to the team's recreated scorecard. Click **"Return to Input View"** to instantly return.

**Golf Match™** compares each team with every other team, and generates total financial results per team member as well as for the team as a whole. A "Results Summary" schedule is also generated which shows, at a glance, all team versus team scores and financial results.

---

## Threesomes vs. Foursomes?

When a group is not divisible into equal foursomes, one, two or three threesomes can result. i.e. 18 players = 3 foursomes and 2 threesomes. So how does everyone compete?

### 1) Using a Ghost

One alternative is to draw a player from another team -- a Ghost -- for each threesome. This alternative then poses two options: 1) the Ghost is considered to be on two teams and wins or loses based upon the aggregate results of the two teams. (The Ghost then has almost twice the normal "action."), or 2) Alternatively, each Threesome merely uses the scores of a Ghost and the Threesome pays or collects the "action" of a foursome, thus increasing the "action" of each threesome member by 33%.

### 2) Threesomes within Foursomes

**Golf Match™** also provides an additional alternative which has a certain advantage. In the above example of 18 players, (3 foursomes and 2 threesomes), the competition between the 3 foursomes would be calculated on the Foursomes worksheet. Also, however, each foursome would also be competing against the 2 threesomes as follows: each foursome would be considered two "contrived" threesomes, where the A, B and C player would be considered one threesome, and the A, B and D player would be considered the second threesome. Each true threesome would then be competing against 7 other threesomes: the other true threesome plus the two contrived threesomes within each of the three foursomes. The results of these bets would be calculated on the Threesomes worksheet. (Each foursome would divide by four the aggregate results of its two contrived threesomes.)

The advantage of this alternative is that each true threesome will then better know how it is doing during the round, since the threesome is not reliant upon the scores of a Ghost who is playing in another group. In addition, it should be noted that this alternative increases the action of all participants.

And finally, the Threesomes worksheet provides an important accommodation if using the "Threesomes within Foursomes" scenario. Since, in our example, the three foursomes are already competing against each other on the Foursomes worksheet, it would be double jeopardy to have the contrived threesomes within each foursome ALSO be competing against each other. Therefore, the Threesomes worksheet provides a check box option -- "Check teams having 'no bet' with each other" -- which eliminates bets between Threesomes checked and provides a solution to the double jeopardy issue. (Each contrived threesome should be checked.)

---

## Foursomes Round Robin

The "Foursomes Round Robin" worksheet will calculate the results of multiple concurrent Nassau bets (including presses) between up to fifteen foursomes. Merely "check" which Nassaus are being played (one low ball, 2nd low ball, 3rd low ball, 4th low ball -- any or all may be played) between foursomes. Enter bet amounts (front 9, back 9, 18 hole total) and the bet amount and condition for automatic presses, if selected. Also, choose Match or Medal play, and how handicap strokes are allowed. The Foursomes Round Robin also includes the option of comparing Birdies, Eagles and Double Eagles between competing foursomes, net and/or gross. (If contemplating calculating all of this manually, your marriage will soon become a distant memory.)

The number of teams competing and the player numbers for each foursome must be entered manually or alternatively, may be imported from the Foursomes worksheet, if this information has already been entered there. Next, simply press the "Calculate Round Robin" button and get out of the way.

**Golf Match™** sequentially places each Foursome in the "Hot Seat" position and calculates and then posts the financial results versus every other foursome. Once the computer stops churning, the aggregate net results for each team appears on the row which starts with each Team's Name. In addition, a table showing individual results between all teams may be viewed by pressing the "View Results" button. To print results, hit the Excel Print icon. Give the printed results to the Captain of the foursome which wins the most, and let him collect from the Captains of losing teams and pay the Captains of any other winning teams. Captains obviously pay or collect from their respective team members. Bingo; you'll be home in time for the news.

Also, in order to review the hole by hole score card and calculations for any particular match between any two foursomes, enter the Team Number of one of the foursomes into the "hot seat" position. Then simply "click" the opposing Team Name (listed just below the "Hot Seat" position) and a hyperlink will take you to the two adjacent scorecards, showing all hole by hole Nassau bets, including presses as well as results from Birdie, Eagle and Double Eagle comparisons. ("Clicking" the "Freeze Scorecards" button will keep the two score cards frozen while various bets may be scrolled up and down on screen, showing all calculations and results.) When finished, click "Return to Input View" and you're back where you started.

And finally, to print any two foursomes' scorecards and results, follow the above instructions (one team in the "Hot Seat" position, then "click" the opposing Team Name). Next, click the Excel "File" menu, click "Print" and the Excel Print dialogue box will appear. Under "Print what" choose "Selection" then click OK. The two scorecards, all bets and the total results should print. Be sure to follow these steps EXACTLY or you will not be printing what you want.

---

## Skins

A skins game is a hole-by-hole competition between all skins game participants. A skin is won when one player beats (or skins) all other players on any hole. Eighteen "skins" are possible (as in 18 holes, capice?), but any time the low score for a hole is tied, no skin is won. **Golf Match™** generates a report which compares the scores of all skin game participants for each hole, highlights "skins" won, and calculates all amounts won/lost by each player. Two types of skins games are available: 1 -- all players contribute to a skins "pot" (player "buy in" amount multiplied by number of participants) which is divided among the skin winners, or 2 -- all entrants pay each skin winner a specific amount. (In addition, two "Skins" worksheets are included in **Golf Match™** in case a "Gross" skins game and "Net" skins game are played, thus allowing results of both competitions to be saved.)

---

## Indo Tourney

The "Indo Tourney" worksheet facilitates the calculation of an Eighteen Hole Gross and/or Net individual tournament for up to 75 participants. In addition, if desired, Front 9 and Back 9 winners are also calculated within both gross and net divisions. And, up to 15 places plus ties may be paid in all six divisions (Gross-18 Holes, Gross Front 9, Gross Back 9; Net 18 Holes; Net Front 9, Net Back 9). Once everyone's hole-by-hole scores are entered into the Players-Scores worksheet, all results are instantaneous.

---

## Individual Bets

The "Individual Bets" worksheet compares the hole-by-hole scores of two players in either a "match play" or "medal play" Nassau.

If desired, the "Individual Bets" worksheet also provides for automatic "pressing." (A "Press" is merely the start of a new bet, mid-nine-hole-round, that terminates at the end of the particular nine holes being played.) If the "Automatic Presses" box is checked, a new press bet begins when either player falls behind on the most recent bet (original bet or latest press bet) by the pre-determined number of holes (match play) or strokes (medal play).

**Golf Match™** instantly calculates all Nassau and press bets for up to 23 opponents at one time. **Golf Match™** generates a complete hole-by-hole score card for each match (including presses if selected) and calculates the financial outcome of each specific match. (Simply click the Match # to be linked to the scorecard for any match. Then click "Return to Input View" to be instantly returned to where you started.) In addition, **Golf Match™** totals the aggregate financial result for the player whose bets are being calculated, as well as the individual result for each opponent.

In other words, a golf-gamble-aholic can now make up to 23 separate $5 Nassaus, all with automatic-one-down $5 presses, and in seconds, **Golf Match™** will calculate the total net result for the golf-gamble-aholic as well as the individual result for each of his opponents. Hit the Excel Print icon, collect and pay. Kabam.

A second "Individual Bets" worksheet, "Individual Bets (2)", with identical capabilities, is included in Golf Match.

---

## Indo – Round Robin

The "Indo Round Robin" worksheet will calculate the results of a one on one match OR medal play competition between all combinations of up to 23 participants, including automatic presses if desired. Simply enter game parameters: match or medal play; Nassau bet amounts (front 9, back 9, 18 hole total); select auto-presses, when, and amounts (if desired); choose how handicap strokes are allowed; and enter the player numbers for all participants in the Round Robin (up to 23). Next, simply press the "Calculate Round Robin" button and stand back.

**Golf Match™** sequentially calculates and then posts the financial results of all matches. It also totals each participant's net win or loss on the **Indo – Round Robin – RESULTS** schedule. Depending upon the speed of your computer, the process may take 10 seconds to run or possibly a few minutes. (I've never tried it on a slow computer, so I don't really know. Our new laptop pulls it off in about 10 or so seconds.) Results are viewed by pressing the "View Results" button. Make sense? Again, hit the Excel Print icon, hand the printed results to the biggest winner, and let him collect and pay.

In addition, in order to review the hole by hole score card for any particular match, enter the player number of one of the participants into the "hot seat" position, then simply "click" the match number directly above the respective opponent's player number. A hyperlink takes you to the scorecard and clicking "Return to Input View" brings you back to where you started.

---

## Best Ball Bets

In **Golf Match™**, a best ball, four ball, or better ball competition pits one two-man team against one or numerous opposing two-man teams in hole-by-hole Match play competition. For Match Play, the lower score for one team is compared with the lower score for another team on each hole. In a Match Play Nassau bet, three separate bets are made. Each team is betting that it will win more holes on the front nine, on the back nine, and on the total eighteen holes.

If Medal Play (stroke play) is selected, however, the lower score on each hole for one team is totaled for the front nine holes, for the back nine holes, and for all eighteen holes, and is compared with the same three total stroke scores for each opposing team. So a Medal Play Nassau is again three separate bets, but is based upon aggregate strokes per nine and eighteen, instead of holes won per nine and eighteen, as in Match Play.

Matches may be played using Full Handicap (select Full Handicap and input 100%), Gross/no handicap (select Full Handicap and input 0%), or Off the Low Handicap (select OFF THE LOW), where the lowest handicapper plays at gross, and all other players receive strokes based upon the difference between their handicap and the lowest handicap in the foursome. (A 10 handicapper would get strokes on the 6 hardest holes if a 4 was the lowest handicap in the foursome.)

**Golf Match™** calculates the results of one team (Sheet Hangers) competing against up to as many as 46 opposing two-man teams. (We call them "Sheet Hangers" because they hang a sheet of paper with their two names at the top, challenging any and all comers and combinations in a two man Best Ball competition. Without **Golf Match™** these bets used to take forever to figure. Now they take seconds.)

Upon selection of Best Ball Bets, competition type (Match or Medal play), and handicap allocation method; and input of wager amounts and player numbers, **Golf Match™** instantly generates total bet results. In addition, and once again, **Golf Match™** recreates a score card for each match, showing the individual scores for all four players, the hole by hole match as it progresses, and the front nine, back nine and 18 hole results. To view the scorecard for any match, simply click the Match #. Then click "Return to Input View" to be instantly returned to where you started.

On the "Best Ball $ W/L" worksheet, **Golf Match™** also tallies financial results for each bet, as well as the aggregate financial result for each player who participates in any of the 46 possible Best Ball matches. (Some guys sign up with multiple partners.) Click on the Excel Print icon on the Standard Excel Menu, the results are printed and the Sheet Hangers can quickly pay or collect one amount from each participant.

In addition, should a review of any match be requested, the "Best Ball $ W/L" worksheet also provides a hyperlink, to the scorecard for each match by clicking on the Team #. And again, to return, simply click "Return to Best Ball $ W/L Sheet."

---

## Combo Bets

The "Best Ball Bets" worksheet also provides the ability to calculate best ball combo bets where all best ball combinations of up to 4 players are compared and calculated against all best ball combinations of up to 4 other players. **Golf Match™** quickly calculates the following combo bets:

- **"Wheel"** -- 2 players competing against all combos of 3 other players -- 3 bets
- **"Rope"** -- 2 players competing against all combos of 4 other players -- 6 bets
- **"Igg"** -- all combos of 3 players vs 3 players -- 9 bets
- **"Big Wheel"** -- all combos of 3 players vs 4 players -- 18 bets
- **"Big Igg"** -- all combos of 4 players vs. 4 players -- 36 bets

First, choose Combo Bets. Then choose Competition Type (Match or Medal Play), Handicap preference, bet amounts, Combo Bet type (Wheel, Rope, Igg, etc.), enter player numbers for Team #1 and Team #2, and simply press the "Calculate Combo Bets" button. Boom; it's done. Click "View Combo Bet Results" and immediately click the Excel Print icon on the Standard Excel Menu. To return, click "Return to Input View."

---

## Best Ball Bets (2)

A second "Best Ball Bets" worksheet, "Best Ball Bets (2)," is also included in **Golf Match™**. "Best Ball Bets (2)" has identical capabilities as the "Best Ball Bets" worksheet, except a) it does not include Combo Bets, and b) it does provide for automatic "presses." (As discussed above under Individual Bets, a "Press" is merely the start of a new bet, mid-nine-hole-round, that terminates at the end of the particular nine holes being played.) If the "Automatic Presses" box is checked, a new press bet begins when either team falls behind on the most recent bet (original bet or latest press bet) by the pre-determined number of holes (match play) or strokes (medal play). The additional "Best Ball Bets (2)" worksheet is also followed by a "Best Ball $ W/L (2)" worksheet, which generates the result for each bet (including applicable presses), as well as the aggregate financial results for all participants in the "Best Ball Bets (2)" worksheet. (Oftentimes we have more than one set of Sheet Hangers, so a second "Best Ball Bets" worksheet is handy.)

---

## Gross and Net Best Ball Totals

The "Best Ball Bets (2)" worksheet can also be used to calculate eighteen hole best ball gross and net totals for up to 47 two man teams (Sheet Hangers plus all 46 opponent teams). At the top of the worksheet, merely select the "FULL HANDICAP or % of FULL HANDICAP" option, and enter the percentage used (some best ball tournaments are played with less than full handicap). **Golf Match™** will then generate gross and net best ball results for each two man team. To view scores, click any Team # >> and you'll be hyperlinked to the respective scorecard; scroll to the end of the scorecard for Gross and Net scores. Again, to return to where you started, click the "Return to Input View" button.

---

## Best Ball -- Round Robin

The "Best Ball--Round Robin" worksheet will calculate the results of a four ball (best ball) Match or Medal Play competition between all combinations of up to 23 teams, including auto-presses. Simply enter game parameters: match or medal play; Nassau bet amounts (front 9, back 9, 18 hole total); select auto-presses, when, and amounts (if desired); choose how handicap strokes are allowed; and enter the player numbers for each two-man team in the Round Robin (up to 23 possible teams). Next, simply press the "Calculate Round Robin" button.

Just like the Indo-Round Robin, **Golf Match™** sequentially calculates and then posts the financial results of all matches to the **Four Ball (Best Ball) -- Round Robin -- RESULTS** schedule. This schedule shows the team by team results as well as aggregate team results and individual team member aggregate results.

And once again, depending upon the speed of your computer, calculations may take a few seconds to run or possibly a few minutes. Results are viewed by pressing the "View Results" button. Again, hit the Excel Print icon, hand the printed results to the biggest winners, and let them collect and pay. Then click "Return to Input View" in order to return to input view. Make sense?

Also, and once again, in order to review the hole by hole score card for any particular match, enter the player numbers for one two man team into the "hot seat" positions, then "click" the team number directly above the respective opposing team. Whoosh. There's the card, the scores, the hole by hole match. To return, as always, click the "Return to Input View" button.

---

## Grand Total

The Grand Totals worksheet accomplishes its name exactly. It provides a Grand Total for each golfer from the information residing in all of the other **Golf Match™** worksheets. **Golf Match™** also provides the ability to select or unselect which worksheets to include in the Grand Total generated.
